comment.body:
depends on how the model works, and what strategy you mean...
so if the gamma is very high on a day and the profile is full of these local gamma nodes, you can still sell premium, but can't really trade direction.
only momentum-wise, to filter out days, it is almost impossible, i think, bcs momentum is very conditional, very context-dependent. 

so, directional intraday trading can be avoided when price action is messy and you can't have a clear read of what the market wants, and say it doesn't worth the risk..
or it also can be avoided if gamma is expected to be sold heavily across the strikes...

So I think, there is no answer for that.
market is stochastic.

i say sometimes that "i don't see intraday edge", i usually explain why... but if you would ask me every day, why i said that, you would get differenet answers

comment.body:
Thanks Alma. 

I was inspired by your indicators you share with us to build an automated day-trading strategy using them + some other filters. One thing im curious about is trying to figure out days where daytrading is just not ideal. You've mentioned more than a few times that there are days where you just dont see any edge in trading.
Is it possible to compute that (fairly) easily?

comment.body:
yepp... 
in heavily gamma-sold days, i go for reversion trades, but only if there is a decent room for the momentum to breath. otherwise the spread, the fees and the taxes will eat your profit.
so if there is no room for a move, i don't touch it. i can be wrong, but i don't risk unnecessary 
and even i may sell a put or call (the opposite of my directional trade)
